body.title: Document NE force-run / stew salience coupling invariant (0.6 / 0.65)
body.result: Appended INVARIANT block to theigors/subsystem_index/narrative_engine: 'stew salience (0.65) > NE force-run threshold (0.6)' constraint with file:line cites.
body.description:
narrative_engine.py:477 force-runs NE only when an observation has salience >= 0.6. ebook_reader.py:745 + 4 other sites deposit at salience 0.65 specifically so they reliably trigger force-run. This invariant (stew salience strictly > NE force-run threshold) is undocumented — change either value and stew chunks silently stop integrating into LTM.

DONE 2026-04-27: Appended INVARIANT block to theigors/subsystem_index/narrative_engine palace node naming both values, both source lines, and the constraint.

Affected files:
- clan.memory_palace path='theigors/subsystem_index/narrative_engine' (UPDATE: appended INVARIANT block).
- (No code change — invariant already correct in code.)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: invariant lives in palace node now; future sprints touching either value must read the NE subsystem node first.
- postgres-only: ✓.
- test-plan-or-why-not: no automated test (constraint check would have to trace constants across 5+ files; cost > value). Manual: a /day-close-audit step could grep for the two constants and warn if they invert.

Scope boundary:
IN: palace doc of the invariant.
OUT: refactoring to a shared CONSTANT (would be T-ne-stew-threshold-as-constant — file separately if useful), automated invariant check.

Test plan:
- SELECT from palace shows the INVARIANT block at the bottom of the NE node.
- Verified 2026-04-27.

Follow-up candidate (not blocking): hoist both values into a shared module so one source change covers all callers.
